title: Batrachochytrium dendrobatidis electroporation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"75vjv","text":"Batrachochytrium dendrobatidis (BD) is a parasitic symbiote with many frogs. BD infection is devastating to most frog species, causing mass mortality in ~6.5% of amphibian species. To understand the cellular biology of BD and its interaction with amphibians, we have developed an method of electroporation, allowing for efficient deliver of extracellular constructs to infectious BD zoospores. The current method reliably loads 95% of treated cells with extracellular dextrans.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"6o9jd","text":"Keep everything cold and work quickly when off ice. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fddp0","text":"Dispose of supernatant as quickly as possible as zoopores will swim up from out of the pellet after centrifugation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Make 50 mL SM buffer (SM buffer should not be more than 7 days old).

SM Buffer: 5 mL 50 millimolar (mM), KCL (Autoclaved) 750 µL pH 7.2 1 Molarity (M), Sodium Phosphate Buffer (Filter Sterilized) 7.5 mL 100 millimolar (mM), MgCL2 (filter sterilized) .338 g, Sodium Succinate dibasic acid hexahydrate .228 g, D-Mannitol 36.75 mL, Sterile water
Make an appropriate volume of Paraformaldehyde fixation buffer.

Make 2mg/mL dextrans in SM buffer ( Be sure it is Cat No. D3305 from Invitrogen by Thermo Fisher Scientific) dextrans is sticky, measure in the tube you will be adding buffer to. Spin down at high speed, then use supernatant.
Measure out the required amount of autoclaved growth media into an appropriate flask.
Place fixation buffer, growth media, and bonner's salts on ice.
Transfer synced cells to 50mL conicals
Resuspend in 10 mL of SM buffer, combine after resuspension if multiple tubes were needed to hold all the synced spores.
2500 rpm @ 4 °C for 00:05:00 . Discard supernatant immediately.
Turn Centrifuge on to 4 °C
Resuspend in 5mL of SM
Spin down cells and discard supernatant 2500 rpm, °C @ 4 °C for 00:05:00
2500 rpm @ 4 °C for 00:05:00 . Discard supernatant immediately.
Resuspend cells in the appropriate volume of SM buffer needed for the # of cuvettes and tubes that will be used ( 100 µL SM for each cuvette)
Count cell concentration. Aiming for 2x10 7 - 1x10 7 cells/mL
If the spores are overly concentrated, dilute to the appropriate concentration.
Prepare cuvettes & tubes. E- = electroporation control (no voltage) D- = Dextrans control (no dextrans)
Prepare E-D- tubes. 1.5mL eppendorfs: 100 µL, SM 100 µL, Cell suspension
Preprare E-D+ tubes: 1.5ml eppendorfs 100 µL, Dextrans solution 100 µL, Cell suspension
Prepare E+D- cuvettes: 0.2cm electroporation cuvettes 100 µL, SM 100 µL, Cell suspension
Prepare E+D+ cuvettes: 0.2cm electroporation cuvettes 100 µL, Dextrans solution 100 µL, Cell suspension
Set samples on ice for ~ 10 minutes
Set electroporation protocol
Squarewave protocol 1000V 2 pulses 5 sec intervals 2mm cuvette
Insert cuvette
Hit the PULSE button, record percent droop
Remove cuvette and place immediately on ice.
00:10:00, allow samples to rest on ice
Add 200 µL, chilled growth media to each cuvette
Gently pipette liquid in cuvette up and down to mix cells just before electroporation
00:10:00, allow samples to rest on ice
Transfer contents of each tube/cuvette to 15mL conicals.
5 mL, chilled growth media to each sample
2500 rpm 4 °C 00:05:00 discard supernatant
go to step #27 Unless supernatant has been discarded three times.
add 5 mL, Bonner's salts
2500 rpm 4 °C 00:05:00 discard supernatant
go to step #30 until supernatant as been discarded 2 times
Aliquot 100 µL cells for imaging
Aliquot 100 µL cells for counting
Fix remaining cells for flow cytometry
Repeat steps 18-21 for all cuvettes, be sure to pipette control eppendorf tubes even though they will not be exposed to a pulse.
add 600 µL, Bonner's salts 4 °C
Add an equal volume of fixation buffer (see go to step #2 ) to each remaining volume of cells.
Immediately place tubes on ice. 00:15:00 (centrifugation time in the following step is IN ADDITION to this time). Leave alone for 15 minutes.
Centrifuge 2200 rpm, °C, @ Room temperature 00:05:00
Properly dispose of supernatant and resuspend in 400 µL, Bonner's salts
Immediately quantify via flow cytometry or wrap in foil and store at 4 °C for up to 4 hours.
Final Concentration of PFA fixation buffer: 4.8% PFA 9mM Sucrose 50mM Sodium Phosphate Buffer, pH 7.2
creator: Andrew Swafford
